edge: SparseFusion is a sparse view 3D reconstruction approach that unifies recent advances in neural rendering and probabilistic image generation.
SparseFusion unifies recent advances in neural rendering and probabilistic image generation.
SparseFusion is a sparse view 3D reconstruction approach that builds on neural rendering with re-projected features.
SparseFusion shows that geometric consistency and generative inference can be complementary.
SparseFusion demonstrates that geometric consistency and generative inference can be complementary in a mode-seeking behavior towards probabilistic image generation.

Our approach learns a (conditional) diffusion model that can infer detailed plausible novel-view images.
While this probabilistic image synthesis approach allows the generation of higher quality image outputs, it does not directly yield a 3D representation of underlying the object.
To obtain a consistent 3D representation, we propose a Diffusion Distillation technique that ‘distills’ the predicted distributions into an instance-specific 3D representation.
The conditional diffusion model not only gives us the ability to sample novel-view images but also to (approximately) compute the likelihood of a generated one.
Using this insight, we optimize an instance-specific (neural) 3D representation by maximizing the diffusion-based likelihood of its renderings.

We demonstrate our approach on over 50 real-world categories from the CO3D, which results in more accurate and realistic renderings, while also recovering a 3D-consistent representation of the underlying object.

Extracting 3D Modes via Diffusion Distillation with the proposed VLDM.
The proposed VLDM does not output a 3D representation, so the challenge is to obtain an output 3D representation while preserving rendering quality.
VLDM predictions can be distilled to obtain a 3D neural scene representation such as NeRF, to approximate the likelihood of a generated novel view.
VLDM predictions can be distilled to obtain a 3D neural scene representation such as Instant NGP (INGP), to approximate the likelihood of a generated novel view.
Both NeRF and Instant NGP (INGP) are proposed as instance-specific 3D neural scene representations fθ.
The renderings x ≡ fθ(π) from arbitrary viewpoints π should be likely under the conditional distribution modeled by the VLDM pϕ(x
Neural Mode Seeking is a process that encourages a representation maximizing likelihood, while Diffusion Distillation involves a reconstruction objective for computing the likelihood of a data point.

The reconstruction objective in Diffusion Distillation yields a bound on the log-likelihood of a data point, which is essential for computing the likelihood of an image to be used in mode-seeking optimization.
The distortion model predicts a latent representation of the image, serving as a key step in the mode-seeking optimization process.
The behavior of mode-seeking versus mean-seeking is visualized to show the difference in optimizing for representation likelihood versus minimizing distance to samples.

In the practice of multi-step denoising and image-space reconstruction, several modifications are made to the single-step objective for improved performance, including loss in pixel space, perceptual distance consideration, and adaptive use of multiple time steps.
